title: tests: do not use registry.fedoraproject.org images
body:
It looks like registry.fedoraproject.org was down for a bit last Friday at least causing many test to fail.

We should avoid depending on external resources as much as possible for reliable CI testing.
There are two problems with that.
First, the image on the remote could change or be removed at any time because we do not own it which would break the tests. IMO we should only ever use images under the quay.io/libpod namespace that we control.
Second, pull from the internet is always prone to many flakes, the image should be cached in a local registry to avoid failures during registry outages. We have a working cache registry setup in cirrus and this has been working really well in podman (https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/22726)

comments.body:
I agree reliable CI is valuable.

I think we need to have _some_ tests for the c/image registry against “real” registries that run in production, not stubs/fakes, and also in the default no-mirrors-involved configuration.

Now,
- That definitely doesn’t mean that we have to run against external projects that have no motivation to keep servers up for our CI. It would be much better to run against an intentionally-chosen curated list of servers that matter, instead of relying on, e.g.,  whatever `fedoraproject.org` chooses to deploy this month.
- We don’t need to run _all_ tests against production-like registries; just enough to exercise the client code
- c/image testing really should happen in the container-libs repo, the reliance on Skopeo’s tests is really all wrong.
Short-term https://github.com/containers/skopeo/pull/2740 , although that doesn’t help if the registry is really down.

yeah I don't think the retry would have helped, I saw tests failing everywhere on Friday but sure better than nothing.

I do agree real testing against real registry is important as this is how most users use us. But does that need to be inside PRs? And then still there is the problem we rely on that specific image to stay as is, images we don't control are not suitable test images long term. We have been broken countless of times because external images changed in ways the break tests. And if it happens it is no just fix up main and move on, it has to be backported into the supported branches (i.e. RHEL) as well which can be a lot of extra work.

So if we do run against real registries it should always use images that we own, i.e. quay.io/libpod. So if we want to test other registries we should have account for them to have our own specific test images there so we know they won't change suddenly.

> But does that need to be inside PRs?

I don’t think testing against realistic servers is very different from any other tests that run on PRs — perhaps the server changes and breaks us, perhaps we change `c/image/docker` and break it. The latter case should almost certainly be found immediately in the PR; the former would be best found when we decide to update our own “curated list of servers” to a new version … a PR on _some_ repo.

We could do it in nightlies… with the usual issues of nightlies, they are easy to ignore, and they come late.

I’m fully supporting the _idea_ of a well-designed registry interoperability test. As for whether it’s a current priority … it’s not something I want to work on this year. Perhaps someone else has the capacity.

---

> So if we do run against real registries it should always use images that we own

That’s somewhat in conflict with the desire to cover more server implementations… (ideally we would run our own instances, sure).

There’s also, almost certainly, some low-hanging fruit in reducing the number/size/count of images we pull from these registries. I don’t think there’s a very good reason that `test-integration` should take over 10 minutes, and IIRC there are a few tests that are responsible for a large part of that.

Again, I would like this to be improved but I’m not going to invest into that in the short-term.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
